name: Tomsk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the snows of Siberia and the silver mineral wealth of the region
the taiga forests of the oblast
the rivers Ob and Tom, and the sky
wealth and the historical gold-mining tradition (in the coat of arms)

description: A vertical pentaband of white, green, blue, green and white with the broad blue panel in the centre flanked by narrow green stripes and outer white panels; the coat of arms of Tomsk Oblast is placed at the centre.
history: The flag of Tomsk Oblast was adopted in 1997. The colour scheme reflects the natural environment of southern Siberia and the historical Tomsk Governorate.

features.symbolism: the central blue panel flanked by narrow green stripes and broader white stripes recalls the snowy taiga of Siberia, the forests of the Tomsk region and the great rivers (the Ob and Tom) flowing through it.
the silver horse on a green field, the historical arms of Tomsk.
